div.p:
DEK Data Encryption Key
k3s A Lightweight Kubernetes Distro
k8s Kubernetes
KEK Key Encryption Key
HSM Hardware Security Module
TPM Trusted Platform Module

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 572px; height: 1px; padding-top: 410px; margin-left: 225px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; max-height: 170px; overflow: hidden;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: none; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
div.h1: Case: 3 k3s server nodes in HA & Network HSM
div.p: In this situation, KEK keys are safely stored on TPMs on each of the three k8s server nodes. Each k8s server node needs to have the same KEK. Indeed, the "Raft leader" cluster randomly generates a DEK seed (32 bytes) which will be stored as an encrypted object in its ETCD database. Then the content of the ETCD DB is synchronised and propagated to the other k8s server nodes.

If one server node (e.g. the Raft leader) crashes and fails, then other server nodes will need to be able to decrypt the DEK in case of cluster reboot. Therefore, each cluster node needs to have access to the same KEK.

switch.text: Case: 3 k3s server nodes in HA & Network HSM...
